title: Service-area / geocoding using open data only (Census/TIGER PD; OSM ODbL attribution)
context: Helping a user find their water system requires geocoding an address or location to a public water system service area. This must use only open/public-domain/ODbL-licensed sources: US Census/TIGER for boundary data (public domain) and OSM/Nominatim for address lookup (ODbL, requiring share-alike attribution). Derived geodata that incorporates OSM must be licensed ODbL. Geocoding maps to water system (pwsId), never to a person — precise user geolocation is never stored. The ADR (wqe-arch-001) must be consulted for the agreed geocoding approach.
objective: Build the service-area/geocoding dataset and lookup module using only open/PD/ODbL data: (1) US Census/TIGER shapefiles for service-area boundaries (public domain); (2) OSM/Nominatim for address-to-coordinate lookup (ODbL, with attribution and share-alike honored); (3) derived geodata that incorporates OSM data licensed ODbL; (4) geocoding result maps to water system pwsId, never to an individual person; (5) precise user geolocation is never stored or logged; (6) ODbL attribution obligations documented and surfaced in the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses only open/PD/ODbL sources; no proprietary geocoding APIs (no Google Maps, MapBox paid tier, or similar)
ODbL attribution and share-alike obligations honored: any derived geodata incorporating OSM data is licensed ODbL
Attribution to Census/TIGER (public domain) and OSM/Nominatim (ODbL) documented in the repo and surfaced in the app
Geocoding maps to water system (pwsId), not to an individual person
Precise user geolocation is never stored, logged, or transmitted beyond the immediate lookup request
Geocoding approach follows the ADR (wqe-arch-001) decision
output: Dataset: service-area geodata (data/geocoding/) built from Census/TIGER and OSM/Nominatim, with attribution, license documentation, and a geocoding lookup module in the pipeline.
